file_structure.description: Kubernetes Namespace for database operators and instances
Kubernetes Namespace for sigma1 services and shared resources
Manifests for CloudNative-PG operator deployment
Manifests for Redis operator deployment
CloudNative-PG cluster definition for sigma1-postgres
Redis operator cluster definition for sigma1-valkey
Terraform configuration for Cloudflare R2 bucket 'sigma1-assets'
Terraform configuration for R2 access credentials
Cloudflare Tunnel configuration for 'morgan' service
ConfigMap containing infrastructure connection details

scaffolds.test_stubs:
#!/bin/bash

# Test stub for Core Infrastructure Setup

echo "--- Verifying Kubernetes Namespaces ---"
kubectl get ns databases && kubectl get ns sigma1

echo "--- Verifying PostgreSQL and Valkey Deployments ---"
kubectl get pod -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=sigma1-postgres
kubectl get pod -n databases -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=sigma1-valkey

echo "--- Verifying ConfigMap 'sigma1-infra-endpoints' ---"
kubectl get configmap sigma1-infra-endpoints -n sigma1 -o yaml

echo "--- Placeholder for S3/R2 and Cloudflare Tunnel verification ---"
# Manual verification steps:
# 1. Check Cloudflare R2 dashboard for 'sigma1-assets' bucket.
# 2. Verify R2 credentials allow read/write access (e.g., using 's3cmd' or 'rclone').
# 3. Check Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard for 'morgan' tunnel status.
# 4. Attempt to reach a placeholder service via the tunnel.

echo "Infrastructure setup verification complete (manual steps required for S3/R2 and Tunnel)."

# Example of connecting to PostgreSQL (requires kubectl port-forward or similar)
# echo "Attempting to connect to PostgreSQL..."
# kubectl port-forward svc/sigma1-postgres-rw -n databases 5432:5432 &
# PGPASSWORD=$(kubectl get secret sigma1-postgres-user -n databases -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
# psql -h localhost -p 5432 -U sigma1_user -d sigma1 -c "SELECT 1;"
# kill %1

# Example of connecting to Valkey (requires kubectl port-forward or similar)
# echo "Attempting to connect to Valkey..."
# kubectl port-forward svc/sigma1-valkey -n databases 6379:6379 &
# redis-cli -h localhost -p 6379 PING
# kill %1


# Expected output for success:
# namespace/databases created
# namespace/sigma1 created
# pod/sigma1-postgres-0 Running
# pod/sigma1-valkey-0 Running
# configmap/sigma1-infra-endpoints exists with expected data

# For Terraform, run 'terraform plan' and 'terraform apply' and verify outputs.
# For Cloudflare Tunnel, use 'cloudflared tunnel status <TUNNEL_NAME>' and check Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.

exit 0

scaffolds.test_stubs:
package integration_test

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"net"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"google.golang.org/grpc"
	"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure"
	"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb"

	pb "github.com/sigma1/rms-service/pkg/api/rms/v1"
	"github.com/sigma1/rms-service/internal/db"
	"github.com/sigma1/rms-service/internal/server"

	"google.golang.org/grpc/test/bufconn"
	"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
	gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Assume setupTestDB and bufDialer from previous task are available or re-defined.

func startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t *testing.T, testDB *gorm.DB) {
	lis = bufconn.Listen(bufSize)
	s := grpc.NewServer()
	repo := db.NewRepository(testDB)
	rmsServer := server.NewRMSServer(repo) // This server should now include new service implementations
	pb.RegisterOpportunityServiceServer(s, rmsServer)
	pb.RegisterProjectServiceServer(s, rmsServer)
	pb.RegisterInventoryServiceServer(s, rmsServer)
	pb.RegisterCrewServiceServer(s, rmsServer)
	pb.RegisterDeliveryServiceServer(s, rmsServer)

	go func() {
		if err := s.Serve(lis); err != nil && err != grpc.ErrServerStopped {
			t.Errorf("gRPC server failed to serve: %v", err)
		}
	}()
}

func TestGetStockLevelGRPC(t *testing.T) {
	testDB := setupTestDB(t)
	startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t, testDB)

	ctx := context.Background()
	conn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, "bufnet", grpc.WithContextDialer(bufDialer), grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	defer conn.Close()

	client := pb.NewInventoryServiceClient(conn)

	productID := "prod-abc"
	// Insert some initial stock level into the database for productID
	// For example: testDB.Create(&db.InventoryTransaction{ProductID: productID, Quantity: 10, Type: "initial"})

	req := &pb.GetStockLevelRequest{ProductId: productID}
	res, err := client.GetStockLevel(ctx, req)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotNil(t, res)
	assert.Equal(t, int32(10), res.StockLevel) // Assuming 10 was inserted
}

func TestRecordInventoryTransactionGRPC(t *testing.T) {
	testDB := setupTestDB(t)
	startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t, testDB)

	ctx := context.Background()
	conn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, "bufnet", grpc.WithContextDialer(bufDialer), grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	defer conn.Close()

	client := pb.NewInventoryServiceClient(conn)

	req := &pb.RecordTransactionRequest{
		Transaction: &pb.InventoryTransaction{
			ProductId:       "prod-xyz",
			ProjectId:       "proj-123",
			Type:            pb.TransactionType_TRANSACTION_TYPE_CHECK_OUT,
			Quantity:        -2,
			RecordedByUserId: "user-a",
			TransactionTime: timestamppb.Now(),
			Notes:           "Checked out for project",
		},
	}

	res, err := client.RecordTransaction(ctx, req)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotNil(t, res)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Id)
	assert.Equal(t, "prod-xyz", res.ProductId)

	// Verify stock level update (e.g., by calling GetStockLevel or querying DB directly)
}

func TestListCrewGRPC(t *testing.T) {
	testDB := setupTestDB(t)
	startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t, testDB)

	ctx := context.Background()
	conn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, "bufnet", grpc.WithContextDialer(bufDialer), grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	defer conn.Close()

	client := pb.NewCrewServiceClient(conn)

	// Insert some test crew members into the database
	// testDB.Create(&db.CrewMember{Name: "John Doe", Email: "john@example.com", Status: "AVAILABLE"})

	req := &pb.ListCrewRequest{Status: pb.CrewStatus_CREW_STATUS_AVAILABLE}
	res, err := client.ListCrew(ctx, req)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotNil(t, res)
	assert.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(res.CrewMembers), 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "John Doe", res.CrewMembers[0].Name)
}

func TestScheduleCrewGRPC(t *testing.T) {
	testDB := setupTestDB(t)
	startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t, testDB)

	ctx := context.Background()
	conn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, "bufnet", grpc.WithContextDialer(bufDialer), grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	defer conn.Close()

	client := pb.NewCrewServiceClient(conn)

	crewMemberID := "crew-1"
	startTime := timestamppb.Now()
	endTime := timestamppb.New(time.Now().Add(2 * time.Hour))

	req := &pb.ScheduleCrewRequest{
		CrewMemberId: crewMemberID,
		EventName:    "Project Alpha Shift",
		StartTime:    startTime,
		EndTime:      endTime,
		Notes:        "Morning shift for Alpha project",
	}

	res, err := client.ScheduleCrew(ctx, req)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotNil(t, res)
	assert.True(t, res.Success)
	// In a stubbed scenario, calendar_event_id might be empty or a placeholder
}

func TestScheduleDeliveryGRPC(t *testing.T) {
	testDB := setupTestDB(t)
	startExtendedTestGRPCServer(t, testDB)

	ctx := context.Background()
	conn, err := grpc.DialContext(ctx, "bufnet", grpc.WithContextDialer(bufDialer), grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	defer conn.Close()

	client := pb.NewDeliveryServiceClient(conn)

	req := &pb.ScheduleDeliveryRequest{
		Delivery: &pb.Delivery{
			ProjectId:     "proj-456",
			Type:          pb.DeliveryType_DELIVERY_TYPE_DELIVERY,
			Address:       "123 Main St, Anytown",
			ScheduledTime: timestamppb.New(time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour)),
			Status:        pb.DeliveryStatus_DELIVERY_STATUS_SCHEDULED,
			ItemIds:       []string{"item-a", "item-b"},
		},
	}

	res, err := client.ScheduleDelivery(ctx, req)
	assert.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotNil(t, res)
	assert.NotEmpty(t, res.Id)
	assert.Equal(t, "proj-456", res.ProjectId)
}

func TestUpdateDeliveryStatusREST(t *testing.T) {
	// This test requires the REST gateway to be running and accessible.
	// Placeholder for actual HTTP request.
	t.Skip("REST gateway integration test for delivery status not fully implemented yet.")
}
